message:
feat(engine): prepared DML dispatch, B-tree balance hardening, pager improvements, and parser extensions

Comprehensive engine improvements across multiple crates:

Core (connection.rs, compat_persist.rs):
- Introduce PreparedDmlDispatch enum to separate precompiled DML execution
  (using cached bytecode + minimal dispatch metadata) from deferred AST-based
  execution, reducing memory overhead for frequently-executed INSERTs
- Add PagerBackend::page_size() accessor for cross-backend page size queries
- Refine compat persistence layer with improved error context

B-tree (balance.rs, cursor.rs):
- Harden balance algorithm with additional overflow/underflow edge case handling
  across 308 lines of changes for more robust page splitting and merging
- Fix cursor navigation edge case in btree traversal

Pager (pager.rs, arc_cache.rs):
- Refine ARC cache eviction logic for better hit rates under mixed workloads
- Improve pager page management with 297 lines of changes covering page
  lifecycle, dirty tracking, and write-back coordination

MVCC (invariants.rs, physical_merge.rs):
- Extend MVCC invariant checks with additional consistency validations
- Refine physical merge logic for concurrent writer scenarios

Parser (parser.rs, token.rs):
- Extend parser with 115 lines of improvements for more complete SQL coverage
- Add new token type support

E2E & Harness:
- Enhance fsqlite_executor with 167 lines of changes for better test coverage
- Extend verification gates with 220 lines of new validation logic
- Add reporting infrastructure improvements across bench_summary, benchmark,
  report, and report_render modules
- Update MVCC concurrent writers test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+
+    #[test]
+    fn test_behavioral_phase_gate_commands_do_not_target_marker_tests() {
+        let gate_plan = phase_4_to_6_gate_plan()
+            .into_iter()
+            .chain(phase_7_to_9_gate_plan())
+            .collect::<Vec<_>>();
+
+        for gate in gate_plan {
+            let command = gate.command.join(" ");
+            assert!(
+                !command.contains("test_phase4_gate_")
+                    && !command.contains("test_phase5_gate_")
+                    && !command.contains("test_phase6_gate_")
+                    && !command.contains("test_phase7_gate_")
+                    && !command.contains("test_phase8_gate_")
+                    && !command.contains("test_phase9_gate_"),
+                "gate {} still targets a marker/self-check command: {}",
+                gate.gate_id,
+                command
+            );
+        }
     }
 
     #[test]
